summary: Regression check after app.json deployment-config fix (added ios.bundleIdentifier='com.nova.app', android.package='com.nova.app', removed missing googleServicesFile). No regressions. Backend: 24/24 pytest cases pass (100%) on backend_test.py; all target endpoints (/api/auth/login, /api/auth/me, /api/conversations, /api/stories, /api/calls, /api/friends/requests, /api/locations/friends) return 200 for an authenticated Alice; /api/register-push returns 201 {status:'skipped'} with valid payload (placeholder EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY, degrades gracefully) and 422 with meaningful validation errors when required fields (user_id/platform/device_token) are missing. Frontend (mobile web preview @ 375x667): app boots without red screen or JS errors, login screen renders with Nova branding + email/password + Google; email/password login as alice@nova.app succeeds and lands on tabbed shell (Story/Chat/Calls/Locations/Settings). Chat tab lists conversations, Bob DM opens, sending a new message ('TEST_iter3_<ts>') succeeds and immediately appears in the thread. Push registration effect is gated by Platform.OS==='web' return-early in AuthContext, so removing googleServicesFile does not affect the running web preview; on native it will call POST /register-push which is confirmed non-blocking (201 skipped). Only console output was a benign 'navigator.vibrate blocked before user gesture' warning — not related to the config change.
critical_code_review_comments: app.json: bundleIdentifier and android.package now set to 'com.nova.app' — resolves V3MobileConfigInvalid. googleServicesFile correctly omitted (file not present); FCM push will not be usable in a native Android build until a real google-services.json is added, but this does not affect web preview or the register-push endpoint (which returns skipped with placeholder key).
AuthContext.tsx L157: push registration effect returns early on Platform.OS==='web', so the web build never touches expo-notifications native APIs — safe.
Login flow still uses correct EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL; JWT stored and used across tabs; no regressions observed after config change.
seed_data_creation: None new. Reused alice@nova.app / bob@nova.app. One test message TEST_iter3_<ts> left in Alice↔Bob DM (safely prefixed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression-only iteration — no code changes made. Backend base: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL + /api. Web preview at https://nova-orbit-1.preview.emergentagent.com. Test users alice@nova.app / bob@nova.app (password123). register-push requires user_id, platform, device_token; returns 201 {status:'skipped'} with placeholder EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY. On native Android, real FCM will need a google-services.json committed and re-referenced in app.json before EAS build; unrelated to app functionality in preview.
rca_of_issue: N/A — deployment config change (bundleIdentifier/package added, missing googleServicesFile reference removed) is fully backwards-compatible with the running web preview. All target flows unchanged.
